description: 30일 미국 일상 회화 (정중한 표현 + 현지 캐주얼/슬랭 표현 병기)

register_legend.polite: 정중/표준 - 처음 만나는 사람, 격식 있는 자리

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Hey, how are you?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: I'm good, thanks. How about you?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Pretty good. Just busy with work.
dialog_turns.translation: 꽤 괜찮아. 그냥 일이 좀 바빠.

native_variations.note: 가장 흔한 인사. 대답은 'Good, you?' 정도면 충분.
친구끼리. 진짜 안부보다 가벼운 인사. 'Not much, you?'로 답함.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Hi, I'm Alex.

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Nice to meet you, Alex. I'm Jordan.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Nice to meet you too.

native_variations.note: Nice to meet you와 거의 동일, 약간 더 편안함.
온라인 등으로 알다가 처음 직접 만날 때.

dialog_turns.speaker: Barista
dialog_turns.text: Hi, what can I get for you?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Can I get a medium latte, please?

dialog_turns.speaker: Barista
dialog_turns.text: For here or to go?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: To go, thanks.

native_variations.note: 미국 카페에서 가장 흔한 주문 방식.
'I'll do ~'는 주문할 때 원어민이 자주 씀.

native_variations.text: Could I have a latte, please?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Beautiful day, isn't it?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Yeah, finally! It's been raining all week.
dialog_turns.translation: 응, 드디어! 일주일 내내 비 왔잖아.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I know, right? I'm glad it cleared up.

native_variations.note: 맞장구칠 때 미국인이 입에 달고 사는 표현.

lessons.target_expression: Could you tell me how to get to ...?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to the station?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Sure. Go straight and turn left at the light.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Got it. Thank you so much!

lessons.phonetic: kʊd juː tɛl miː haʊ tə ɡɛt tuː

native_variations.text: How do I get to the station?

native_variations.note: 이해했다는 뜻으로 정말 자주 씀. = I understand.

native_variations.text: Would you mind pointing me to the station?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Do you want to grab lunch this weekend?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Sure! Saturday works for me.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Sounds good. Let's say noon?

native_variations.text: You down for lunch this weekend?
Would you like to have lunch together?

dialog_turns.speaker: Server
dialog_turns.text: Are you ready to order?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Yes, I'll have the cheeseburger, please.

dialog_turns.speaker: Server
dialog_turns.text: Anything to drink?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Just water for now, thanks.

native_variations.note: 주문 시 'I'll do ~'를 원어민이 자주 씀.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Hey, is now a good time?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Actually, I'm in a meeting. Can I call you back?
dialog_turns.translation: 사실 회의 중이야. 다시 전화해도 될까?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Of course. Talk later.

native_variations.text: Can I hit you back in a bit?
Now's not great—I'll call you back.
May I call you back shortly?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Excuse me, how much is this?

dialog_turns.speaker: Clerk
dialog_turns.text: That one's twenty dollars.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Okay, I'll take it.

native_variations.note: 구매 결정 시 정말 자주 쓰는 표현.

native_variations.text: Could you tell me the price of this?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: So, what do you do?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: I work in marketing. How about you?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I'm a software developer.

native_variations.text: What do you do for work?
What line of work are you in?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I think we should leave early to beat traffic.
dialog_turns.translation: 차 막히기 전에 일찍 출발해야 할 것 같아.

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: I agree. Let's go around seven.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Works for me.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Sorry I'm late! Traffic was crazy.

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: No worries, you're fine.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Thanks for understanding.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Thanks for helping me move!

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Of course! Anytime.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I owe you one.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I gotta run. See you later!

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Okay, take care!

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: You too!

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: What do you do for fun?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: I'm really into hiking these days.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Nice! We should go sometime.

native_variations.text: I'm kind of a hiking nerd.
I enjoy hiking in my free time.

lessons.target_expression: Can you do me a favor?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Hey, can you do me a favor?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Sure, what's up?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Could you water my plants while I'm away?
dialog_turns.translation: 나 없는 동안 화분에 물 좀 줄 수 있어?

lessons.phonetic: kæn juː duː miː ə ˈfeɪvər

native_variations.text: Can you do me a solid?
native_variations.note: 'do me a solid' = 부탁 들어주다 (매우 캐주얼).

native_variations.text: Would you mind doing me a favor?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Excuse me, where's gate B12?

dialog_turns.speaker: Staff
dialog_turns.text: Go down this hall and take a right.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Great, thanks. Is the flight on time?

native_variations.text: Could you direct me to gate B12?

lessons.target_expression: I'd like to place an order.

dialog_turns.speaker: Staff
dialog_turns.text: Thanks for calling Mario's. How can I help?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Hi, I'd like to place an order for pickup.

dialog_turns.speaker: Staff
dialog_turns.text: Sure, what would you like?

lessons.phonetic: aɪd laɪk tə pleɪs ən ˈɔrdər

native_variations.text: Can I place an order to go?
I'd like to place an order for delivery.

lessons.target_expression: There seems to be a problem.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Excuse me, I think there's a problem with my order.
dialog_turns.translation: 실례합니다, 제 주문에 문제가 있는 것 같아요.

dialog_turns.speaker: Staff
dialog_turns.text: Oh, I'm so sorry. What happened?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: I ordered the salad, but I got fries.

lessons.phonetic: ðɛr siːmz tə biː ə ˈprɑbləm

native_variations.text: I think there's been a mix-up.
This isn't quite what I ordered.
Sorry to bother you, but could you check this?
native_variations.translation: 번거롭게 해서 죄송한데, 이것 좀 확인해 주실래요?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I love your jacket! Where'd you get it?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Oh, thanks! I got it on sale.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: It looks great on you.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: How's your day going?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Honestly, kind of rough. Long day at work.
dialog_turns.translation: 솔직히 좀 힘들어. 직장에서 긴 하루였어.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Aw, hang in there. Almost the weekend.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I'm starving. Why don't we order pizza?
dialog_turns.translation: 나 너무 배고파. 우리 피자 시킬까?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Good idea. Let's do it.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Cool, I'll order now.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: We're having a little get-together Friday. You should come!
dialog_turns.translation: 금요일에 조촐하게 모임 있어. 너도 와!

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: I'd love to! What time?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Around eight. Bring a friend if you want.

native_variations.text: We'd love to have you join us.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Do you like spicy food?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Honestly, I'm not a big fan of spicy stuff.
dialog_turns.translation: 솔직히 매운 건 별로 안 좋아해.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: No problem, let's get something mild.

native_variations.text: I'm not really into spicy food.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Excuse me, does this bus go downtown?

dialog_turns.speaker: Driver
dialog_turns.text: It does. Three stops from here.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Perfect, thank you!

native_variations.text: Is this the right train for downtown?
native_variations.translation: 시내 가는 거 이 기차 맞아요?

native_variations.text: Could you tell me if this bus goes downtown?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: You okay? You look a little pale.

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: I'm not feeling well. I think I'm coming down with something.
dialog_turns.translation: 몸이 안 좋아. 뭔가 감기 기운이 오는 것 같아.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: You should go home and rest.

native_variations.note: 병이 시작되는 느낌일 때 흔한 표현.

native_variations.text: I'm afraid I'm a bit under the weather.
native_variations.translation: 몸이 좀 안 좋은 것 같아요.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Wanna catch a movie tonight? LMK

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Sounds good! brb, checking my schedule.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: np, take your time.

native_variations.note: 잠깐 자리 비울 때 채팅 약어.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Should we split the bill?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Yeah, let's split it. I'll Venmo you.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Cool, no rush.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I'm so nervous about my interview tomorrow.

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Don't worry, you've got this!
dialog_turns.translation: 걱정 마, 넌 할 수 있어!

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Thanks, that means a lot.

native_variations.text: I have every confidence in you.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Hey! It's been a while. How have you been?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: I know! It's been way too long. I've been great.
dialog_turns.translation: 그러니까! 진짜 너무 오랜만이야. 난 잘 지냈어.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: We should catch up over coffee soon.

native_variations.note: 'catch up' = 못 본 사이 근황 나누다.

native_variations.text: It's been too long. We must catch up.

dialog_turns.speaker: Interviewer
dialog_turns.text: So, tell me a little about yourself.
dialog_turns.translation: 자, 본인에 대해 간단히 말씀해 주세요.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Sure. I have five years of experience in marketing.
dialog_turns.translation: 네. 저는 마케팅 분야에서 5년 경력이 있습니다.

dialog_turns.speaker: Interviewer
dialog_turns.text: Great. Why are you interested in this role?
dialog_turns.translation: 좋네요. 이 직무에 관심을 가진 이유는요?

native_variations.text: I'm a great fit for this role.
native_variations.translation: 저는 이 직무에 딱 맞는 사람입니다.

native_variations.text: I'd welcome the opportunity to contribute.

lessons.target_expression: Is there any room to negotiate?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: I'm really interested, but is there any room to negotiate on the price?
dialog_turns.translation: 정말 관심 있는데, 가격 협상의 여지가 좀 있을까요?

dialog_turns.speaker: Seller
dialog_turns.text: We could do a small discount if you buy today.
dialog_turns.translation: 오늘 구매하시면 약간 할인해 드릴 수 있어요.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Could you meet me halfway at $400?

lessons.phonetic: ɪz ðɛr ˈɛni rum tə nɪˈɡoʊʃieɪt

native_variations.text: Can you cut me a deal?
Would you consider a lower price?

lessons.target_expression: Where do you see yourself in five years?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Where do you see yourself in five years?
dialog_turns.translation: 5년 후 본인은 어떤 모습일 것 같아요?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Honestly, I'd love to be leading my own team by then.
dialog_turns.translation: 솔직히 그때쯤엔 제 팀을 이끌고 있으면 좋겠어요.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: That's a solid goal. What's your plan to get there?

native_variations.text: I'm working toward a management role.
My long-term goal is to start my own business.
native_variations.translation: 장기 목표는 제 사업을 시작하는 거예요.

native_variations.text: I aspire to take on more responsibility.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I can't decide whether to take the job.
dialog_turns.translation: 그 일을 받아들여야 할지 못 정하겠어.

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: If I were you, I'd go for it.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: You think so? Maybe you're right.

native_variations.text: You might want to think it over.
native_variations.translation: 곰곰이 생각해 보는 게 좋을 것 같아.

native_variations.text: Have you considered all your options?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I think we should launch next week.

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: I see your point, but I think we need more testing.
dialog_turns.translation: 무슨 말인지 알겠는데, 테스트가 더 필요한 것 같아요.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Fair enough. Let's talk it through.
dialog_turns.translation: 일리 있네요. 좀 더 얘기해 봅시다.

native_variations.text: I get where you're coming from, but...
I'm not so sure about that.
With respect, I'd have to disagree.

lessons.target_expression: I'd like to book a room.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Hi, I'd like to book a room for two nights.

dialog_turns.speaker: Staff
dialog_turns.text: Of course. A single or a double?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: A double, please. Is breakfast included?

lessons.phonetic: aɪd laɪk tə bʊk ə rum

native_variations.text: Do you have any rooms available?
I'd like to make a reservation.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: How much is the rent per month?

dialog_turns.speaker: Landlord
dialog_turns.text: It's $1,500, utilities not included.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Is there a security deposit?

native_variations.text: Are utilities on top of that?
Could you tell me the lease terms?

lessons.target_expression: I'd like to open an account.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Hi, I'd like to open a checking account.

dialog_turns.speaker: Teller
dialog_turns.text: Sure! Do you have a photo ID?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Yes, here's my driver's license.

lessons.phonetic: aɪd laɪk tə ˈoʊpən ən əˈkaʊnt

native_variations.text: I need to deposit a check.
I'd like to set up an account, please.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Do you have something for a sore throat?
dialog_turns.translation: 목 아픈 데 먹는 거 있나요?

dialog_turns.speaker: Pharmacist
dialog_turns.text: Sure, these lozenges work well.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Great. How often should I take them?

native_variations.text: What do you recommend for a cold?

native_variations.translation: 이거 처방전 없이 살 수 있어요?
native_variations.note: 'over the counter' = 처방전 없이 구입 가능한.

dialog_turns.speaker: Lead
dialog_turns.text: Alright, let's get started. First on the agenda is the budget.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Before we move on, could I add a quick point?
dialog_turns.translation: 넘어가기 전에 한 가지만 짚어도 될까요?

dialog_turns.speaker: Lead
dialog_turns.text: Of course, go ahead.

native_variations.text: Let's circle back to that later.
Let's touch base after the meeting.
Shall we proceed to the next item?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: So, what brings you to the conference?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: I'm here to connect with people in the industry.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Same here. Let's exchange contacts.

native_variations.text: I'd love to connect on LinkedIn.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Did you hear about the new policy?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Yeah, it's been all over the news. What do you make of it?
dialog_turns.translation: 응, 뉴스에 도배됐더라. 넌 어떻게 생각해?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Honestly, I'm on the fence about it.
dialog_turns.translation: 솔직히 난 아직 입장을 못 정했어.

native_variations.text: I'm on the fence about it.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Have you seen that new series everyone's talking about?
dialog_turns.translation: 다들 얘기하는 그 새 시리즈 봤어?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Not yet, but it's on my list. Is it any good?
dialog_turns.translation: 아직, 근데 볼 거 목록에 있어. 괜찮아?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: It's a must-watch. You'll binge it.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Did you catch the game last night?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Yeah! What a comeback in the last minute.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I know, I was on the edge of my seat.

native_variations.text: It came down to the wire.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: My laptop keeps freezing. It's not working.
dialog_turns.translation: 노트북이 자꾸 멈춰. 작동을 안 해.

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Have you tried turning it off and on again?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Yeah, but it didn't help.

native_variations.text: Could you take a look at it?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Hi, I'd like to return this. It doesn't fit.
dialog_turns.translation: 안녕하세요, 이거 반품하고 싶어요. 사이즈가 안 맞아요.

dialog_turns.speaker: Staff
dialog_turns.text: No problem. Do you have the receipt?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Yes, and can I get a refund?

native_variations.text: Can I exchange this for a bigger size?
native_variations.translation: 더 큰 사이즈로 교환할 수 있어요?

native_variations.text: Is it too late to return it?
I'd like to request a refund, please.

lessons.target_expression: How often do you work out?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: How often do you work out?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: I try to hit the gym three times a week.
dialog_turns.translation: 일주일에 세 번은 헬스장 가려고 해.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Nice, I should get back into it.

lessons.phonetic: haʊ ˈɔfən duː juː wɜrk aʊt

native_variations.text: I hit the gym after work.
I'm trying to get in shape.
native_variations.note: 'get in shape' = 몸 상태를 좋게 만들다.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Do you have any pets?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Yeah, I have a golden retriever named Max.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Aw, I love dogs! How old is he?
dialog_turns.translation: 아 강아지 너무 좋아! 몇 살이야?

native_variations.text: I've had her since she was a puppy.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: So, are you seeing anyone these days?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Actually, yeah. We just started dating.
dialog_turns.translation: 사실 응. 우리 막 사귀기 시작했어.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: That's awesome! How did you two meet?

native_variations.text: We hit it off right away.
We've been seeing each other for a while.
I'm not really dating at the moment.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Do you have any siblings?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Yeah, I have an older brother and a younger sister.
dialog_turns.translation: 응, 형(오빠) 한 명이랑 여동생 한 명 있어.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Nice, are you guys close?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: There's a storm warning for tonight.

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Really? We should stock up on water just in case.
dialog_turns.translation: 정말? 혹시 모르니 물 좀 사둬야겠다.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Good call. Let's stay indoors.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: You've been working nonstop. You okay?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Honestly, I'm burned out. I need to recharge.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Take some time off. You've earned it.

native_variations.text: I need to unplug for a bit.
I'm taking some time off to rest.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: We have a long weekend. Where should we go?

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: How about a road trip up the coast?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I'm in! Let's map out a route.

native_variations.text: Should we book it in advance?

lessons.target_expression: I'd like to make a reservation.

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Hi, I'd like to make a reservation for two at seven.

dialog_turns.speaker: Staff
dialog_turns.text: Sure, may I have your name?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: It's under Kim. Thank you!

lessons.phonetic: aɪd laɪk tə meɪk ə ˌrɛzərˈveɪʃən

native_variations.text: Can I book a table for two?
I'd like to reserve a table, please.

dialog_turns.speaker: Tourist
dialog_turns.text: Excuse me, how do I get to the museum?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: Go down this street and it's on your right.
dialog_turns.translation: 이 길 따라 내려가면 오른쪽에 있어요.

dialog_turns.speaker: Tourist
dialog_turns.text: Is it walking distance?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: I wanted to let you know I've decided to move on.
dialog_turns.translation: 제가 이직(퇴사)을 결정했다는 걸 알려드리고 싶었어요.

dialog_turns.speaker: Boss
dialog_turns.text: I'm sorry to hear that. When is your last day?

dialog_turns.speaker: You
dialog_turns.text: I'll give two weeks' notice.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I got the job!

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Congratulations! That's amazing news!

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Thanks! I'm so excited.

lessons.target_expression: I'm so sorry for your loss.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: I heard about your grandmother. I'm so sorry for your loss.
dialog_turns.translation: 할머니 소식 들었어. 정말 마음이 아파(애도해).

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Thank you. It means a lot.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Let me know if there's anything I can do.

lessons.phonetic: aɪm soʊ ˈsɑri fɔr jər lɔs

native_variations.text: If you need anything, I'm here.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Remote work is clearly more productive.

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: I'd argue that it depends on the person.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: That's a fair point, but the data says otherwise.
dialog_turns.translation: 일리 있는데, 데이터는 그 반대를 말해.

native_variations.note: 합의가 안 될 때 부드럽게 마무리.
반박 전에 자기 말 들어달라고 할 때.

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: You won't believe what happened to me today!
dialog_turns.translation: 오늘 나한테 무슨 일이 있었는지 믿기지 않을걸!

dialog_turns.speaker: B
dialog_turns.text: Oh no, what happened?

dialog_turns.speaker: A
dialog_turns.text: Long story short, I locked myself out.

native_variations.note: 긴 얘기를 줄여 핵심만 말할 때.
흥미로운 얘기 꺼낼 때 주의 끌기.

native_variations.text: To make a long story short, ...
